pageTimings.text: Flint had decided to map every single path in Moonwood. He had a new roll of parchment and a good quill. He started at the Hollow Oak and walked north.
He drew as he walked. He mapped the Crystal Stream path. He mapped the way to Tumblerock Cliffs. He drew everything carefully.
He walked for three days. He did not stop. His map grew longer and longer. It was full of detail.
On the fourth day, Flint held up his finished map. "There," he said. "Every path in Moonwood. All of it."
He looked around to show someone. He was alone. The forest around him looked very different to the forest on his map.
He checked his map. According to the map, he was standing in the Whispering Meadow. He was not in the Whispering Meadow. The paths had moved.
Flint sat down on a mossy rock. He looked at the map for a long time. It was very beautiful. It was also completely wrong.
Flint took out a new piece of parchment. "I'll make another one," he said. He was not upset about this. He had always known the paths would move.
By the time Flint found his way home, he was, as usual, slightly lost. The new map was already three pages long. He had taken a very interesting route.
"How was the mapping?" asked Pip. "Perfect," said Flint. "I know exactly where everything was."
Flint pinned the first map up inside the Hollow Oak. It was very beautiful. Everyone agreed it was the best wrong map they had ever seen.
Then Flint started on the next map. He was already very happy. Outside, the paths rearranged themselves quietly. They always did.
